description:
# Offline-First Core Architecture

Implement comprehensive offline-first architecture with local data persistence, queued operations, and transparent sync status. The app remains fully functional without internet connectivity, automatically syncing when connection is restored.

## Rationale
Addresses critical user pain point: 'Lack of offline access to travel plans when internet is unavailable.' Also addresses competitor pain points: TripIt server downtime (pain-1-1), Wanderlog sync errors (pain-2-3), and general travel app offline functionality issues (gap-2). This is foundational for travelers in remote areas, flights, or regions with poor connectivity.

## User Stories
- As a solo traveler, I want to access my trip plans offline so that I can view my itinerary during flights or in remote areas without internet
- As a traveler, I want the app to automatically save my changes locally so that I don't lose data if my connection drops
- As a user, I want clear indicators of my sync status so that I know when my data is safe in the cloud

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] App remains fully functional without internet connection
- [ ] All data changes are queued and sync automatically when connection is restored
- [ ] Clear visual indicators show offline/online status and sync progress
- [ ] Local database persists all user data, trips, journals, and preferences
- [ ] Conflict resolution handles concurrent edits gracefully
- [ ] Users can read, create, edit, and delete all data types while offline

phases.name: Phase 1: Foundation & Dependencies
phases.description: Set up the foundational dependencies and project structure for offline-first architecture

subtasks.title: Add Local Storage Dependencies
subtasks.description: Add sqflite and drift packages for local SQLite database with type-safe queries
subtasks.notes:
Successfully added sqflite and drift dependencies to pubspec.yaml:
- sqflite: ^2.3.0 added to dependencies
- drift: ^2.14.0 added to dependencies  
- sqlite3: ^2.4.0 added to dev_dependencies
- drift_dev: ^2.14.0 added to dev_dependencies

All dependencies added in appropriate sections following the project's existing structure. Changes committed with hash bfc3f06.

subtasks.title: Add Connectivity & Path Provider Dependencies
subtasks.description: Add packages for network connectivity monitoring and file system access
subtasks.notes:
Successfully added path_provider: ^2.1.0 and path: ^1.8.0 to pubspec.yaml dependencies. Verified connectivity_plus: ^6.1.3 was already present. These packages enable:
- path_provider: Access to app's documents directory for SQLite database storage
- path: Cross-platform path manipulation utilities
- connectivity_plus: Network connectivity monitoring (already existed)

Changes committed with hash acee064.

subtasks.title: Create Offline Core Module Structure
subtasks.description: Create directory structure for offline/sync feature following clean architecture
acceptanceCriteria: Barrel files (export index) created in each directory
subtasks.notes:
Successfully created complete directory structure for offline/sync feature following clean architecture:
- lib/features/offline/data/datasources with offline_datasources.dart
- lib/features/offline/data/models with offline_models.dart
- lib/features/offline/data/repositories with offline_repositories.dart
- lib/features/offline/domain/entities with offline_entities.dart
- lib/features/offline/domain/repositories with offline_repositories.dart
- lib/features/offline/domain/services with offline_services.dart
- lib/features/offline/infrastructure/database with offline_database.dart
- lib/features/offline/infrastructure/sync with offline_sync.dart
- lib/features/offline/presentation/providers with offline_providers.dart
- lib/features/offline/presentation/widgets with offline_widgets.dart

All barrel files created with appropriate exports. Structure follows existing patterns from auth and profile features. Changes committed with hash 050b51f.

subtasks.title: Create Offline Core DI Module
subtasks.description: Create dependency injection module for offline/sync services
acceptanceCriteria: OfflineModule created with providers for database, sync queue, and sync manager
subtasks.notes:
Successfully created dependency injection module for offline/sync services:

1. Created lib/app/di/modules/offline_module.dart:
   - Phase 1: Registered Connectivity singleton for network monitoring
   - Phase 2-8: Added comprehensive TODO placeholders for all future services
   - Includes detailed documentation for each phase's dependencies
   - Follows the same pattern as auth_module.dart and core_module.dart

2. Updated lib/app/di/service_locator.dart:
   - Imported offline_module
   - Added registerOfflineModule call in _registerFeatureModules

The module is now ready to be populated with actual service registrations as each phase is implemented. The structure provides a clear roadmap for all offline-first dependencies.

phases.name: Phase 2: Local Database Layer
phases.description: Implement the local SQLite database with Drift ORM for data persistence

subtasks.title: Design Database Schema
subtasks.description: Define database tables for trips, journals, users, sync queue, and sync metadata
acceptanceCriteria: Trips table defined with all necessary fields
Journals table defined with foreign key to trips
Users table defined for caching user data
SyncQueue table defined for pending operations
SyncMetadata table defined for tracking sync state
subtasks.notes:
Successfully created comprehensive Drift database schema with 5 tables:

**Created Tables:**
1. **Trips table** - Complete with all trip fields plus sync tracking (isSynced, hasPendingChanges, version, isDeleted, lastSyncedAt)
2. **Journals table** - With foreign key to trips, includes content, mood, location, images, and sync fields
3. **Users table** - Caches user profile data locally with sync support
4. **SyncQueue table** - Tracks pending sync operations with priority, retry logic, and status
5. **SyncMetadata table** - Monitors sync state per entity type

**Key Features:**
- All data tables include sync-specific fields for offline-first operations
- Strategic indexes defined for common query patterns (user trips, trip journals, pending sync operations, etc.)
- Proper primary keys and foreign key relationships
- Comprehensive documentation for all fields
- Follows Drift ORM conventions with @DataClassName annotations

**File Created:**
- lib/features/offline/infrastructure/database/schema.dart (371 lines)
- Updated lib/features/offline/infrastructure/database/offline_database.dart (added export)

**Commit:** efb2bf6

Database version migration system set up

phases.name: Phase 3: Network Monitoring

acceptanceCriteria: Debouncing to prevent rapid status changes

subtasks.title: Create Connectivity State Provider
subtasks.description: Create Riverpod provider to expose connectivity state to the app

subtasks.title: Implement Network Reachability Testing
subtasks.description: Add actual network testing beyond basic connectivity checks
acceptanceCriteria: Actual HTTP request to test API reachability
Cached result with TTL to avoid excessive tests

phases.name: Phase 4: Sync Queue System
phases.description: Implement operation queue for tracking and syncing offline changes

subtasks.title: Define Sync Operation Types
subtasks.description: Create enum and models for sync operations (create, update, delete)

subtasks.title: Create Sync Queue Repository
subtasks.description: Implement repository for managing sync queue in local database

subtasks.title: Create Sync Queue Service
subtasks.description: Implement service to manage sync queue lifecycle

subtasks.title: Implement Operation Interceptors
subtasks.description: Create interceptors that capture write operations and queue them

phases.name: Phase 5: Data Synchronization Engine
phases.description: Implement bidirectional synchronization with conflict resolution

subtasks.title: Create Sync Manager
subtasks.description: Implement core sync coordinator that orchestrates all sync operations

subtasks.title: Implement Upload Sync (Local → Server)
subtasks.description: Implement syncing queued operations to server
acceptanceCriteria: Update local database with server IDs
Retry on failure with exponential backoff

subtasks.title: Implement Download Sync (Server → Local)
subtasks.description: Implement syncing server data to local database
acceptanceCriteria: Query for server changes since last sync

subtasks.title: Implement Conflict Resolution
subtasks.description: Create strategy for handling concurrent edit conflicts
acceptanceCriteria: Detect version conflicts (server changed since last sync)
Manual resolution option for complex conflicts

subtasks.title: Implement Incremental Sync
subtasks.description: Add incremental sync based on timestamps/version vectors
acceptanceCriteria: Track last synced timestamp per entity type
Query only changed data from server
Full sync fallback if delta fails

subtasks.title: Create Sync Status Provider
subtasks.description: Create provider to expose sync status and progress to UI

phases.name: Phase 6: Repository Adaptation
phases.description: Adapt existing repositories to work with offline-first pattern

subtasks.title: Create Offline-Aware Base Repository
subtasks.description: Create base repository class with offline-first logic
acceptanceCriteria: Abstract base repository with common sync logic
Fallback to API when local unavailable

subtasks.description: Update TripRepository to use offline-first pattern

subtasks.title: Adapt Profile/User Repository

subtasks.title: Implement Optimistic UI Updates
subtasks.description: Add optimistic response handling for immediate UI feedback
